rulings.Case Title: PEGASTAFF, A DIVISION OF PEGASOFT CORPORATION, A VS. CORESTAFF SERVICES, LP, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 1, 2013, Line 23, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION Ntc Of Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings. Defendant California Public Utilities Commission's ("CPUC") motion for a judgment on the pleadings is granted as to the second and third causes of action. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Public Utilities Code Section 1759 states that "[n]o court of this state, except the Supreme Court and the court of appeal, to the extent specified in this article, shall have jurisdiction to review, reverse, correct, or annul any order or decision of the commission or to suspend or delay the execution or operation thereof, or to enjoin, restrain, or interfere with, the commission in the performance of its official duties, as provided by law and the rules of court." (Pub. Util. Code § 1759.) The plain language of Public Utilities Code Sections 8281-8286 shows that the California Legislature's intended for Defendant CPUC's job duties to include the procurement of contracts to women, minority, and disabled veteran business enterprises. (Pub. Util. Code §§ 8281-8286.) In response, the CPUC promulgated General Order 156, the purpose of which was to "implement PU Code Sections 8281-8286 which require the Commission to establish a procedure for gas, electric and telephone utilities ¿ and to submit annual detailed and verifiable plans for increasing women, minority and disabled business enterprises' procurement in all categories." (See California Public Utilities Commission GO 156, Section 1.1.1.) Pursuant to Section 1759, this Court lacks jurisdiction to review constitutional challenges to Sections 8281-8286 because it would interfere with Defendant CPUC's job duties. (See Pub. Util. Code. § 1759.) This Court also lacks jurisdiction under Section 1759 to hear constitutional challenges to GO 156 because the trial court lacks jurisdiction to review, reverse, correct, or annul any order or decision of the commission. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RAK)

Jones Law Group, In The Amount Of $2500
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, July 1, 2013, Line 3: Defendants HOWARD R. MULLIN, individually and dba Trustee of The Mullin Family Trust Dated Oct 1, 1982 and SUSAN MULLIN, individually and dba Trustee Of The Mullin Family Trust Dated Oct 1, 1982'S Motion for an Order to Compel the Deposition of The Person(S) Most Knowledgeable of BRPS LLC and Production of Documents Thereat and for Monetary Sanctions against BRPS LLC and Its Attorneys of Record, Shannon B. Jones Law Group, in the Amount of $2500 Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. No Tentative Ruling, except that defendants Howard and Susan Mullin's motion for sanctions is denied. Among the matters to be addressed by counsel at the hearing the following should be addressed. 1.The defendants seek to compel the deposition of the PMK of BRPS LLC, apparently a Canadian corporation with no apparent presence in CA and the US. PUREG was a California corporation which became a sub of BRPS LLC and was dissolved after having cross complained against defendants. Please clarify these corporate relationships. 2. Please address the applicable law in light of these corporate relationships and of the fact that PUREG had cross complained in this matter. 3. Please address the legal merits of the BRPS refusal to furnish a PMK for its discovery request. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAEED J IBRAHIM VS. EXTREME AUTO RECOVERY COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 1, 2013, Line 4, PLAINTIFF SAEED IBRAHIM's Motion To Amend First Amended Complaint. The Court denies the motion to amend as to Plaintiff's wife's proposed claims as they are time barred. The Court denies as to the cause of action for unlawful repossession because there is no such cause of action; the tort is conversion, and in this case the Defendant did not obtain the car by unlawful means. In addition, Plaintiff failed to comply with CRC 3.1324 and did not provide a declaration explaining the delay in bringing this motion. The motion to amend as to claim for civil extortion is denied, without prejudice, because Plaintiff failed to comply with CRC 3.1324. rulings.Case Title: JANNICKE KOLDERUP et al VS. OKLS, INC., DOING BUSINESS AS OKELL'S FIREPLACE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Andmoton To Compel The Continued Deposition Of Robert Maiolo, For The Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, July 1, 2013, Line 4: Defendant OLKS, INC., doing business as OKELL'S FIREPLACE'S Motion to Compel the Continued Deposition of Robert Maiolo, for the Production of Documents and for Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant OKLS Inc.'s motion to compel the further/continued deposition of cross defendant Robert Maiolo and for sanctions is denied. Cross defendant Maiolo, a self-employed plumber, is a party here under an equitable indemnity cross-complaint. Two depositions of him have already taken place, one noticed by defendant OKLS on July 30, 2012, and the other noticed by plaintiffs on April 23, 2013. Defendant OKLS's stated reasons in its motion papers here for an even further or, to use its terminology, continued, deposition are somewhat opaque, particularily given the extensive discovery opportunities already afforded it by the two earlier depositions of cross defendant Maiolo. Defendant had a long period of time between the two depositions to prepare for the second and to cover in the second all of issues and areas that it needed to, but which it now may be trying to address. In addition, the further deposition requested would, if granted, run afoul of the spirit, if not the precise letter, of the seven hour deposition limit rule of C.C.P section 2025.290. Under the circumstances, there does not appear to be the requisite good cause now to warrant a deviation from that provision's obligations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrianacolley@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (302/JPT)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Compel Pltf Signature To Authorizations To Release Sensitive Records And Information

Moving party has failed to meet its burden of persuasion that Welfare and Institutions Code § 10850 does not protect the records covered by the subpoena and that Welfare and Institutions Code § 10850.2 authorizes this Court to compel Plaintiff to authorize Defendant or its counsel to view the records covered by the subpoena for the purposes sought here. rulings.Case Title: JANE ROE VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Reconvene Depo
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, July 1, 2013, Line 2: Plaintiff JANE ROE'S Motion to Reconvene Deposition Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. No Tentative Ruling except that plaintiff's motion for monetary sanctions is denied. Plaintiff has moved to reconvene the deposition of defendant De Santis primarily to question her about certain documents she may have generated while an employee. Among the matters to be addressed by counsel at the hearing are the following. 1. Whether as a legal matter matter defendant De Santis as an employee would herself have been legally able to produce documents (particularily emails generated by her on the bank's email system) while she was an employee of defendant bank. 2. Further to question no. 1, as a matter of law, were the documents requested "documents in the possession of" Ms.De Santis for the purpose of the discovery request? 3. Is there a different legal result to the above questions when, as here, De Santis is no longer employed by the defendant bank? 4. Please address the effect of the bank's non disclosure agreements with De Santis on her ability to produce the requested documents. 5. Please address the need for the breadth and generality of the document requests given the relatively short time period plaintiff and Ms. De Santis were co workers. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CRAIG GOLD VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 1, 2013, Line 15, PLAINTIFF CRAIG GOLD's Motion For Leave To File Amended Complaint. The motion is off calendar. The moving party failed to provide a proof of service, a copy of the motion in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B, and CCSF has advised the court that the motion was never served. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD WINSLOW VS. FAROUK NAKHUDA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 1, 2013, Line 18, CROSS DEFENDANT EDWARD WINSLOW's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/RAK)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA JEAN ALONZI et al VS. CHRISTOPHER J. COSTELLO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, July 1, 2013, Line 19, DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER COSTELLO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Transferred to Department 501, Real Property Court starting on July 1, 2013, to be heard on July 29, 2013 at 9:30 am. The moving party shall provide courtesy copies of the operative pleading per Local Rule 2.6C to Department 501, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than July 1, 2013. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). =(302/RAK)
